title: Solve problems with line plots
description: Practice solving problems using line plots and data sets.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lengths of alien arms are shown below.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f90dba5cdbc1129d25f2c61ecba4cb0ea3d59c63)

**How many alien arms are longer than $96$ centimeters?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] alien arms
hints:
First, we need to find $96$ cm on the number line.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c32e00c41fc65f61853d76524e099c46df599f0d)
Each dot represents one alien arm. 

So, we need to count all of the dots on the line plot that represent lengths longer than $96$ cm.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56dabb3c29bb6a6a7f29afbc3ad85a876246fa7d)
There are $\blueE6$ dots that represent lengths longer than $96$ cm.   

$\blueE6$ alien arms are longer than $96$ cm.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lengths of cereal boxes are shown below.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31b481b50b36977a78731652ab427465a0fc317c)

**How many cereal box lengths are graphed?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] cereal boxes
hints:
Each dot represents one cereal box. 

So, we need to count all of the dots on the line plot.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/295aea99e796c004b93d83ab8b4542750d92e644)

$3 + 2 + 4 + 2 + 3 = 14$

There are $14$ dots on the graph.  

$14$ cereal box lengths are graphed.

assessment_items.question_content:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/943eabd5b355e47ee527c5c06a336a51a64e228b)

[[☃ input-number 1]] reptiles
hints:

So, we need to count all of the dots on the line plot.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb53a44919c64f6a699417aebf5983dbbfbb4f27)

$4 + 2 + 1 + 3 + 2 = 12$

There are $12$ dots.  

$12$ reptiles' lengths are graphed.

assessment_items.question_content:
The heights of Tim's dolls are shown below.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/255f1f36c184974802873845262cd22a54f2405c)


**How many dolls are $24$ centimeters?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] dolls
hints:
First, we need to find $24$ centimeters on the number line.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb41d33a31c651a0cc1e89e610b47120b5d71a73)

Each dot above $24$ represents one doll that is $24 \text { cm}$.  

So, we need to count all of the dots on the line plot that are $24$ centimeters long.

There are $\blueE3$ dots that represent lengths that are $24$ centimeters.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f937c45951f41c585f2c7f06fbc4211e5894057e)
$3$ dolls are $24$ centimeters.
